name: PickCam
tagline: Auto-reframe any group video around one person
description:
Group dance videos, concerts, school recitals — the moment you actually want is one person, but the frame is everyone. PickCam turns any multi-person video into a fancam of whoever you pick: tap a person once and the app follows them to the end of the clip, reframing every frame around them for 9:16, 1:1, or 16:9.

Everything runs on-device. A Core ML detector (YOLOX-Tiny) works alongside Apple's Vision framework to detect, segment, and track people frame by frame, while a face-embedding model re-identifies the chosen subject after occlusions and re-entries — the hard part of this problem is not finding people, it's not losing the *right* one. A path optimizer then smooths the crop trajectory so the output reads like a camera operator followed the subject rather than a bounding box snapping around them. There is no server and no account; video never leaves the device.

The tracking is editable rather than take-it-or-leave-it: split the timeline into segments to switch subjects mid-video, or drop keyframes and drag/pinch the crop when a detection is missed. Export runs through AVFoundation with Metal-based upscaling for 4K output, and monetization is built in — StoreKit 2 subscriptions for Pro, with a rewarded-ad and daily-quota gate on the free tier.
features: Tap one person in a group video and get a fancam — AI follows them to the end
On-device person detection and face re-identification that survives occlusion and re-entry
Reframe to 9:16, 1:1, or 16:9 for Reels, Shorts, and TikTok
Switch the tracked subject per timeline segment, or correct framing with keyframes
Export up to 4K (HEVC/H.264) with Metal-based quality upscaling

name: Nureumkkot
tagline: Turn flowers you meet into digital pressed keepsakes
description:
Nureumkkot (누름꽃, "pressed flower") is a quiet, offline-first keepsake app for people who notice flowers on a walk. Photograph a bloom and the app lifts it from its background, presses it flat with a paper-grain finish, and files it into a personal collection alongside where and when you found it — no account, no ads, no subscription.

Everything runs on-device: Vision's foreground-instance segmentation cuts the subject out, Core Image renders the pressing, and SwiftData plus a file-backed store keep the originals at full quality while derived images are encoded as alpha-preserving HEIC to keep the library roughly 38% smaller. A 355-entry field guide covers bloom months, habitats, and flower meanings, fully localized across 8 languages.

The social layer is deliberately small: CloudKit sharing lets you bind a "flower bookmark" with one friend and mail a single bloom to their inbox, delivered by silent push. A home-screen widget surfaces one flower from your collection, and a share extension lets you send photos in from Photos.app.
features: Photograph a flower and have it cut out and pressed into a keepsake on-device
Browse your collection with the map, date, and notes of where each flower was found
Look up 355 species for bloom months, habitats, and flower meanings
Decorate pressed flowers into framed cards with handwriting, stickers, and a signature
Share a single flower with one bound friend, delivered to their inbox by push
